title: Roasted Japanese Barramundi Tendon
description: A comforting Japanese dish featuring roasted barramundi and nuka-pickle in a rich tendon broth, served in a copper pot.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the barramundi fillets with salt and pepper.
Heat the sesame oil in a copper pot over medium heat.
Sear the barramundi fillets until browned on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
Remove the barramundi from the pot and set aside.
Add the nuka-pickle, dashi broth, soy sauce, mirin, and sake to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Return the barramundi to the pot and spoon some of the tendon broth over the top of each fillet.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast for 12 minutes.
Garnish with green onions and serve hot.
